name: Dark neutron decay (Fornal-Grinstein)
summary: Postulates that ~1% of free neutrons decay through an exotic channel n -> chi + photon, where chi is a dark particle with mass just below m_n - m_p ~ 1.293 MeV. Explains the ~10 s gap between bottle and beam neutron-lifetime measurements (bottle method counts surviving neutrons; beam method only counts protons in standard beta-decay, missing the exotic channel). Predicts a monochromatic gamma at E ~ 0.7-1.4 MeV from neutron decay; experimental searches have constrained but not closed the parameter space.

addresses_puzzles.note: Explains the 4-sigma beam-vs-bottle discrepancy if BR(n -> chi gamma) ~ 1% and Q-value sits below the photon-energy bounds set by Tang et al. (2018) and Sun et al. (2018). Most of the original parameter space has been excluded by direct gamma-line searches and neutron-star stability arguments.

If chi is stable and constitutes DM, requires m_chi within a narrow window (937.9 - 938.8 MeV) to avoid neutron-star instability (McKeen-Pospelov-Raj 2018, arXiv:1803.04205). Most parameter space ruled out by NS observation.

touches_benchmarks.note: BR ~ 1% predicted to reconcile bottle and beam tau_n; direct search via monochromatic gamma.

If neutron decay to dark sector is allowed, neutron stars would convert to dark matter on cosmologically short timescales unless m_chi is fine-tuned just below m_n. Tight bound from observed 2-solar-mass neutron stars.

new_symmetries: U(1)_B-1 connecting visible and dark sectors
introduces.note: chi mass, mediator mass, mediator coupling.

history: Fornal and Grinstein (2018), 'Dark matter interpretation of the neutron decay anomaly', Phys. Rev. Lett. 120, 191801, arXiv:1801.01124. Tang et al. (2018, PRL 121, 022505) and Sun et al. (2018, PRC 97, 052501) constrained gamma-line searches. McKeen-Pospelov-Raj (2018) excluded most via neutron-star stability. Largely retired but illustrative of a class of dark-baryon proposals.

references.citation: Fornal and Grinstein, 'Dark matter interpretation of the neutron decay anomaly', Phys. Rev. Lett. 120, 191801 (2018)
McKeen, Pospelov, Raj, 'Neutron stars exclude light dark baryons', Phys. Rev. Lett. 121, 061802 (2018)
